content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To    :  Advice File:  Sections 84211, 84216                    Date  :  12/21/83
                                                                        M-83-288

From  :  FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
         Carla Wardlow

Subject:  Reporting interest on loans


         When interest charges on a loan are incurred, they
should not be reported on Schedule B (Loans) of the
campaign disclosure statement.  In addition, such
charges need not be reported as an accrued expense
on Schedule F.  They should only be reported on
Schedule E (Expenditures) when paid.

sections.conclusion: When interest charges on a loan are incurred, they should not be reported on Schedule B (Loans) of the campaign disclosure statement. In addition, such charges need not be reported as an accrued expense on Schedule F. They should only be reported on Schedule E (Expenditures) when paid.
sections.question_synthetic: How should interest charges on loans be reported in campaign disclosure statements?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Interest charges on loans should not be reported on Schedule B or as accrued expenses on Schedule F, but should only be reported on Schedule E (Expenditures) when actually paid.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Internal FPPC memorandum to advice file; no formal question/answer sections but contains clear guidance on reporting requirement. Document references sections 84211 and 84216. Typed memorandum format with subject line and single substantive paragraph containing the ruling.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: How should interest charges on loans be reported in campaign disclosure statements?

CONCLUSION: When interest charges on a loan are incurred, they should not be reported on Schedule B (Loans) of the campaign disclosure statement. In addition, such charges need not be reported as an accrued expense on Schedule F. They should only be reported on Schedule E (Expenditures) when paid.
embedding.first_500_words: State of California Memorandum To : Advice File: Sections 84211, 84216 Date : 12/21/83 M-83-288 From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION Carla Wardlow Subject: Reporting interest on loans When interest charges on a loan are incurred, they should not be reported on Schedule B (Loans) of the campaign disclosure statement. In addition, such charges need not be reported as an accrued expense on Schedule F. They should only be reported on Schedule E (Expenditures) when paid.
embedding.summary: FPPC memorandum addressing the proper reporting of interest charges on loans under campaign disclosure requirements, clarifying that such interest should only be reported on Schedule E when paid, not on Schedule B or as accrued expenses on Schedule F.
